text: What is the purpose of the hydrostatic interlock in the keel of an enclosed lifeboat?

options.text: It prevents the engine from starting out of the water
It prevents release of the hooks until the keel of the boat is fully under water
It automatically pumps out the bilges

explanation: The hydrostatic interlock is a critical safety mechanism designed to prevent the accidental release of lifeboat hooks while the craft is still suspended in the davits; it only allows release once the buoyancy of the water triggers the mechanism.
